response.text: Started with less than a dollar, still moved like I had a plan. Mama said, "Boy, keep your focus." I said, "Watch me turn this sand to land." Cold nights, I would count up my losses. Warm hands from a dream I made. I learned fast every scar got value. Every dead end taught me ways. No handouts, I built my table. No shortcuts, I paid that cost. Now they ask how I stay so stable. I was busy turning pain to sauce. I don’t wait, I go get mine. I don’t fold when the pressure climb. Had to fail just to know my shine. Now my future got my name in line. I was down, now I’m rising, yeah. Eyes up, I see the prize, yeah, yeah. Every step, I’m deciding, yeah. My tomorrow ain’t a maybe. I made my way, yeah, I made my way, yeah. I made my way, floors of my own high place, yeah. I made my way, I built this life, I built this life. Built, built this life. Brick by brick, now I’m up in the light. I built this life. Same block but a new direction. Same face but a stronger mind. Used to doubt every last connection. Now I trust what I built inside. I was hungry, I was learning patience. I was sharp when the pressure pushed. Now my steps got a new foundation. Every move got a measured look. I seen friends get lost in the moment. Seen dreams get cheap for a quick thrill. I stayed locked on the bigger picture. Kept it moving when it got real. If you know me, you know I’m different. I don’t talk if I can’t deliver. I was raised on a real ambition. No, no, no, no, no. Every scar became vision, oh. I was nothing, now I’m something. Hear that truth when I say it. I’m the proof of the hunger. Turned a wish into pavement. I was nothing, now I’m something. Hear that truth when I say it. I’m the proof of the hunger. Apply the work, get paid. I made my way, yeah, I made my way, yeah.
